chunks.text: FAQ: Accounts | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar FAQ: Accounts How do I set or reset my IRIS/EECS account password? See our page on Setting Your Account Password . You may also visit visit the EECS Helpdesk

sword . You may also visit visit the EECS Helpdesk for more information and assistance. How do I get an IRIS/EECS account? There are different types of accounts available in EECS, depending on what your affiliation is. If you need an account for a cl

ur affiliation is. If you need an account for a class you are taking, please visit the instructional website . If you are an EECS faculty, staff, or graduate student, or are otherwise affiliated with the department in a way which does not involve cou

the department in a way which does not involve coursework (visitor, collaborator, etc.), you can request an account by filling out an account request form . New graduate students: By default, your accounts will be created in early August. If you will

ounts will be created in early August. If you will be working with a professor or group in the preceding months, they should obtain an account for you using the normal account request form. MEng and 5th Year Masters students: These students do not us

th Year Masters students: These students do not usually get EECS accounts. If your work with a professor or group requires access to EECS resources, or your professor wants you to have an EECS account, then they should obtain an account for you using

, then they should obtain an account for you using the normal account request form. For more information on EECS departmental accounts, please look at the accounts services section of the IRIS Website. To find out whether or not a specific login name

. To find out whether or not a specific login name is already taken, have someone with an LDAP login/password connect to the EECS Roster Database . Do an Advanced People Search . Under Unix Information, enter the login name, and click Search. If the

n, enter the login name, and click Search. If the login is already in use, you will see the name of the person who has it reserved. If no records are returned, the login name is available. See also How do I get my computer on the network? How do I cl

w do I get my computer on the network? How do I close an EECS account? Account Sponsors and Grant Administrators can request the closure of one of their sponsored accounts one of two ways: Use this online request form , or Send email to idsg-admin@ee

line request form , or Send email to idsg-admin@eecs . Accounts are closed on the last day of each month, and requests must be received by the 15th for closure at the end of the current month. See also Account Procedures Why is my account locked? Acc

o Account Procedures Why is my account locked? Accounts are most often locked when it is repeatedly being accessed without authorization (wrong password, etc). Windows : Windows accounts are set to automatically unlock after 10 minutes as long as you

tomatically unlock after 10 minutes as long as you do not attempt any further logins. Please wait 10-20 minutes before making any further attempts to login again. Helpdesk does not have the ability to manually unlock accounts. UNIX : Unix accounts ar

manually unlock accounts. UNIX : Unix accounts are most often locked when the password is too easy to guess. A warning is given to reset the password yourselves, but if you continually login with this password, the shell will be changed to /bin/badp

s password, the shell will be changed to /bin/badpasswd, which will essentially give a standard message every time you login and you will not be able to use the account. What should I consider when choosing my IRIS username? We encourage people to ch

oosing my IRIS username? We encourage people to choose the same username for their IRIS username as they are using for their CalNet ID and @berkeley.edu email address. Your IRIS username is used for: Your default @eecs.berkeley.edu email address. (Yo

Your default @eecs.berkeley.edu email address. (You can create up to three email aliases but they only work for email.) The login name or username to access various EECS resources, such as: Computers & Printers in the EECS.BERKELEY.EDU Windows domain

& Printers in the EECS.BERKELEY.EDU Windows domain Unix computers in the department that automount your EECS home directory Various web applications and pages (too many to list) PUBLIC VISIBILITY Your username will be part of your public web page URL

username will be part of your public web page URL, which will be http://people.eecs.berkeley.edu/~username . Your default email address may appear on some auto-generated mailing lists. If needed, please contact Helpdesk for further assistance. Prima

ase contact Helpdesk for further assistance. Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboard.com .
